title: High molecular weight, post-translationally modified protein brushes
abstract: Disclosed are protein brushes that mimic mucin in physical and functional characteristics. The protein brushes have a variable number of tandem repeats similar to natural mucin and are modified at a number of their tyrosine residues to introduce brush substituents that mimic the hydrogel nature of mucin.
claims:
Claims We claim: 1. A polypeptide comprising a plurality of tandem repeats of a sequence comprised by a mucin selected from the group consisting of MUCl, MUC2, MUC4, MUC7, MUC5AC and MUC5B, wherein about 20% to about 50% of the serine and threonine amino acids in the tandem repeats have been replaced by tyrosine residues. 2. The polypeptide of claim 1, wherein the mucin is MUC5AC or MUC5B. 3. The polypeptide of claim 1, wherein about 33% to about 50% of the serine and threonine amino acids have been replaced by tyrosine residues. 4. The polypeptide of claim 1, wherein the N-terminal and C-terminal amino acids are cysteine. 5. The polypeptide of claim 1, wherein the polypeptide is selected from the group consisting of MUC5ACL, MUC5ACH, MUC5BL, MUC5BH, MUC5ACL-S, MUC5ACL- LT, MUC5ACL+D, MUC5ACLS-15, pCoil-MUC5ACL-S, pCoil-MUC5ACL-LT, pCoil- MUC5ACL+D, MUC5ACL-S-Cold, GST-MUC5ACL-S, MBP-MUC5ACL-S, ELP 1 : 1 Y:S 10k, ELP 3 : 1 Y:S 10k, and MUC5ACLSS. 6. The polypeptide of claim 5, wherein the polypeptide is selected from the group consisting of MUC5ACL, MUC5ACH, MUC5BL, MUC5BH, MUC5ACL-S, MUC5ACL- LT, and MUC5ACL+D. 7. The polypeptide of claim 5, wherein the polypeptide is selected from the group consisting of MUC5ACLS-15, pCoil-MUC5ACL-S, pCoil-MUC5ACL-LT, and pCoil- MUC5ACL+D. 8. The polypeptide of claim 5, wherein the polypeptide is selected from the group consisting of MUC5ACL-S-Cold, GST-MUC5ACL-S, and MBP-MUC5ACL-S. 9. The polypeptide of claim 5, wherein the polypeptide is selected from the group consisting of ELP 1 : 1 Y:S 10k, ELP 3 : 1 Y:S 10k, and MUC5ACLSS. 10. The polypeptide of claim 5, wherein the polypeptide is MUC5ACL. 11. The polypeptide of claim 1, wherein the number of tandem repeat sequences ranges from about 15 to about 70. 12. The polypeptide of claim 6, wherein the length of the repeat sequence ranges from about 700 Da to about 2 kDa. 13. The polypeptide of claim 1, wherein a plurality of the tyrosine residues have been modified to include a substituent selected from the group consisting of alkyl, alkynyl, aryl, amino, carboxyl, heteroaryl, nitro, sulfate, and polyethylene oxide polymer. 14. The polypeptide of claim 13, wherein the plurality is at least 20% of the tyrosine residues. 15. The polypeptide of claim 13, wherein the plurality is at least 50% of the tyrosine residues. 16. A pharmaceutical composition, comprising a polypeptide of any one of claims 1-15; and a pharmaceutically acceptable carrier. 17. A protein oligomer comprising at least two polypeptide units, wherein the polypeptide units comprise a plurality of tandem repeats of a sequence comprised by a mucin selected from the group consisting of MUCl, MUC2, MUC4, MUC7, MUC5AC and MUC5B; and about 20% to about 50% of the serine and threonine amino acids in the protein oligomer have been replaced by tyrosine residues. 18. The protein oligomer of claim 17, wherein the mucin is MUC5AC or MUC5B. 19. The protein oligomer of claim 17, wherein about 33% to about 50% of the serine and threonine amino acids have been replaced by tyrosine residues. 20. The protein oligomer of claim 17, wherein the N-terminal and C-terminal amino acids are cysteine. 21. The protein oligomer of claim 17, wherein the polypeptide is selected from the group consisting of MUC5ACL, MUC5ACH, MUC5BL, MUC5BH, MUC5ACL-S, MUC5ACL-LT, MUC5ACL+D, MUC5ACLS-15, pCoil-MUC5ACL-S, pCoil- MUC5ACL-LT, pCoil-MUC5ACL+D, MUC5ACL-S-Cold, GST-MUC5ACL-S, MBP- MUC5ACL-S, ELP 1 : 1 Y:S 10k, ELP 3 : 1 Y:S 10k, and MUC5ACLSS. 22. The protein oligomer of claim 21, wherein the polypeptide is selected from the group consisting of MUC5ACL, MUC5ACH, MUC5BL, MUC5BH, MUC5ACL-S, MUC5ACL-LT, and MUC5ACL+D. 23. The protein oligomer of claim 21, wherein the polypeptide is selected from the group consisting of MUC5ACLS-15, pCoil-MUC5ACL-S, pCoil-MUC5ACL-LT, and pCoil-MUC5ACL+D. 24. The protein oligomer of claim 21, wherein the polypeptide is selected from the group consisting of MUC5ACL-S-Cold, GST-MUC5ACL-S, and MBP-MUC5ACL-S. 25. The protein oligomer of claim 21, wherein the polypeptide is selected from the group consisting of ELP 1 : 1 Y:S 10k, ELP 3 : 1 Y:S 10k, and MUC5ACLSS. 26. The protein oligomer of claim 21, wherein the protein is MUC5ACL. 27. The protein oligomer of claim 17, wherein the number of tandem repeat sequences ranges from about 15 to about 70. 28. The protein oligomer of claim 27, wherein the length of the repeat sequence ranges from about 700 Da to about 2 kDa. 29. The protein oligomer of claim 17, wherein the at least two polypeptide units are linked together through a disulfide bond between the N-terminus of one protein and the C- terminus of the other protein. 30. The protein oligomer of claim 29, wherein 2 to 30 polypeptide units are linked together using disulfide bonds. 31. The protein oligomer of claim 17, wherein a plurality of the tyrosine residues have been modified to include a substituent selected from the group consisting of alkyl, alkynyl, aryl, amino, carboxyl, heteroaryl, nitro, sulfate, and polyethylene oxide polymer. 32. The protein oligomer of claim 31, wherein the plurality is at least 20% of the tyrosine residues. 33. The protein oligomer of claim 31, wherein the plurality is at least 50% of the tyrosine residues. 34. A pharmaceutical composition, comprising a protein oligomer of any one of claims 17-33; and a pharmaceutically acceptable carrier. 35. A process for preparing a polypeptide comprising a plurality of tandem repeats of a sequence comprised by a mucin selected from the group consisting of MUC1, MUC2, MUC4, MUC7, MUC5AC and MUC5B, wherein about 20% to about 50% of the serine and threonine amino acids in the tandem repeats have been replaced by tyrosine residues, comprising a. expressing the polypeptide through use of a plasmid in a host cell; and b. isolating the polypeptide from the cell. 36. The process of claim 35, wherein the mucin is MUC5AC or MUC5B. 37. The process of claim 35, wherein about 33% to about 50% of the serine and threonine amino acids in the polypeptide have been replaced by tyrosine residues. 38. The process of claim 35, wherein the polypeptide N-terminal and C-terminal amino acids are cysteine. 39. The process of claim 35, wherein the polypeptide is selected from the group consisting of MUC5ACL, MUC5ACH, MUC5BL, MUC5BH, MUC5ACL-S, MUC5ACL- LT, MUC5ACL+D, MUC5ACLS-15, pCoil-MUC5ACL-S, pCoil-MUC5ACL-LT, pCoil- 

 MUC5ACL+D, MUC5ACL-S-Cold, GST-MUC5ACL-S, MBP-MUC5ACL-S, ELP 1 : 1 Y:S 10k, ELP 3 : 1 Y:S 10k, and MUC5ACLSS. 40. The process of claim 39, wherein the polypeptide is selected from the group consisting of MUC5ACL, MUC5ACH, MUC5BL, MUC5BH, MUC5ACL-S, MUC5ACL- LT, and MUC5ACL+D. 41. The process of claim 39, wherein the polypeptide is selected from the group consisting of MUC5ACLS-15, pCoil-MUC5ACL-S, pCoil-MUC5ACL-LT, and pCoil- MUC5ACL+D. 42. The process of claim 39, wherein the polypeptide is selected from the group consisting of MUC5ACL-S-Cold, GST-MUC5ACL-S, and MBP-MUC5ACL-S. 43. The process of claim 39, wherein the polypeptide is selected from the group consisting of ELP 1 : 1 Y:S 10k, ELP 3 : 1 Y:S 10k, and MUC5ACLSS. 44. The process of claim 39, wherein the polypeptide is MUC5ACL. 45. The process of claim 35, wherein the number of tandem repeat sequences ranges from about 15 to about 70. 46. The process of claim 45, wherein the length of the repeat sequence ranges from about 700 Da to about 2 kDa. 47. A process for preparing a polypeptide comprising a plurality of tandem repeats of a sequence comprised by a mucin selected from the group consisting of MUC1, MUC2, MUC4, MUC7, MUC5AC and MUC5B, wherein about 20% to about 50% of the serine and threonine amino acids in the tandem repeats have been replaced by tyrosine residues, comprising modifying a plurality of the tyrosine residues by Process A Process B wherein X is selected from the group consisting of alkyl, alkynyl, aryl, amino, carboxyl, heteroaryl, nitro, sulfate, and polyethylene oxide polymer; and R is selected from the group consisting of H or alkyl; or a pharmaceutically acceptable salt thereof. 48. The process of claim 47, wherein the mucin is MUC5AC or MUC5B. 49. The process of claim 47, wherein X is selected from the group consisting of ethynyl, phenyl, carboxyl, triazolyl, nitro, sulfate and polyethylene oxide. 50. The process of claim 49, wherein X is triazolyl, and the triazolyl is linked to a galactosyl group via a polyol linker. 51. The process of claim 49, wherein X is phenyl, and the phenyl is substituted with an ami do group. 52. The process of claim 47, wherein R is H. 53. The process of claim 47, wherein the plurality is at least 20% of the tyrosine residues. 54. The process of claim 47, wherein the plurality is at least 50% of the tyrosine residues. 55. A process for preparing a protein oligomer comprising at least two polypeptide units, wherein the polypeptide units comprise a plurality of tandem repeats of a sequence comprised by a mucin selected from the group consisting of MUC1, MUC2, MUC4, MUC7, MUC5AC and MUC5B; and about 20% to about 50% of the serine and threonine amino acids in the protein oligomer have been replaced by tyrosine residues, comprising linking at least two polypeptide units together through a disulfide bond between the N-terminus of one protein and the C-terminus of the other protein. 56. The process of claim 55, wherein the mucin is MUC5AC or MUC5B. 57. The process of claim 55, wherein 2 to 30 polypeptide units are linked together using disulfide bonds. 58. The process of claim 55, wherein the N-terminal and C-terminal amino acids are cysteine. 59. The process of claim 55, wherein about 33% to about 50% of the serine and threonine amino acids have been replaced by tyrosine residues. 60. The process of claim 55, wherein the number of tandem repeat sequences ranges from about 15 to about 70. 61. The process of claim 60, wherein the length of the repeat sequence ranges from about 700 Da to about 2 kDa. 62. The process of claim 55, wherein the polypeptide is selected from the group consisting of MUC5ACL, MUC5ACH, MUC5BL, MUC5BH, MUC5ACL-S, MUC5ACL- LT, MUC5ACL+D, MUC5ACLS-15, pCoil-MUC5ACL-S, pCoil-MUC5ACL-LT, pCoil- MUC5ACL+D, MUC5ACL-S-Cold, GST-MUC5ACL-S, MBP-MUC5ACL-S, ELP 1 : 1 Y:S 10k, ELP 3 : 1 Y:S 10k, and MUC5ACLSS. 63. The process of claim 62, wherein the polypeptide is selected from the group consisting of MUC5ACL, MUC5ACH, MUC5BL, MUC5BH, MUC5ACL-S, MUC5ACL- LT, and MUC5ACL+D. 64. The process of claim 62, wherein the polypeptide is selected from the group consisting of MUC5ACLS-15, pCoil-MUC5ACL-S, pCoil-MUC5ACL-LT, and pCoil- MUC5ACL+D. 65. The process of claim 62, wherein the polypeptide is selected from the group consisting of MUC5ACL-S-Cold, GST-MUC5ACL-S, and MBP-MUC5ACL-S. 66. The process of claim 62, wherein the polypeptide is selected from the group consisting of ELP 1 : 1 Y:S 10k, ELP 3 : 1 Y:S 10k, and MUC5ACLSS. 67. The process of claim 62, wherein the protein is MUC5ACL. 68. A process for a protein oligomer comprising at least two polypeptide units, wherein the polypeptide units comprise a plurality of tandem repeats of a sequence comprised by a mucin selected from the group consisting of MUCl, MUC2, MUC4, MUC7, MUC5AC and MUC5B; and about 20% to about 50% of the serine and threonine amino acids in the protein oligomer have been replaced by tyrosine residues, comprising modifying a plurality of the tyrosine residues by Process A or 

 Process B wherein X is selected from the group consisting of alkyl, alkynyl, aryl, amino, carboxyl, heteroaryl, nitro, sulfate, and polyethylene oxide polymer; and R is selected from the group consisting of H or alkyl; or a pharmaceutically acceptable salt thereof. 69. The process of claim 68, wherein the mucin is MUC5AC or MUC5B. 70. The process of claim 68, wherein X is selected from the group consisting of ethynyl, phenyl, carboxyl, triazolyl, nitro, sulfate and polyethylene oxide. 71. The process of claim 70, wherein X is triazolyl, and the triazolyl is linked to a galactosyl group via a polyol linker. 72. The process of claim 70, wherein X is phenyl, and the phenyl is substituted with an ami do group. 73. The process of claim 68, wherein R is H. 74. The process of claim 68, wherein the plurality is at least 20% of the tyrosine residues. 75. The process of claim 68, wherein the plurality is at least 50% of the tyrosine residues.
